name: Full Progression (Step 0 → Endgame) — Milestone Route
description: Prerequisite route for Full Progression (Step 0 → Endgame), segmented into milestone episodes by the progression-router planner. Each phase closes on a quest/unlock capstone rather than an arbitrary skill number.

steps.phase: Toward Early Game (Tutorial Island through first Lumbridge quests)
steps.instruction: ★ Early Game (Tutorial Island through first Lumbridge quests)
steps.detail: Requirements met — start Early Game (Tutorial Island through first Lumbridge quests).

Auto-opens on first login after account creation, live inside Tutorial Island's starting house (not a separate pre-game screen). Set body type, skin colour and clothing style, then confirm to enter the house. Every choice here is free to redo later via Makeover once off the island, so a speedrun-efficient line spends zero time deliberating.

Inside the starting house. Answer his experience-level question ('brand new' / 'played in the past' / veteran). Answering 'brand new' or 'played in the past' unlocks the Adventure Paths task system, which changes where you spawn in Lumbridge later (see the Lumbridge-arrival step); veteran skips Adventure Paths. Listen through the welcome-to-Gielinor and talk-to-NPCs explanation and his mention of the settings menu. This is the only tutorial dialogue choice with a lasting mainland effect -- both answers complete this step identically, so it is not a fork in the checklist, only in where you land.

hints.note: 'brand new'/'played in the past' unlock the Adventure Paths mainland spawn; veteran does not. Both answers complete the step.

steps.detail: Settings > Display > Graphics. Set camera zoom distance to preference; leave 'Scroll wheel can change zoom distance' enabled for quick manual zoom; optionally set Screen Brightness / Hide roofs. Worth doing now (or during the Guide's settings mention) rather than mid-instruction, since zoom/roofs affect visibility of instructors and objects in Tutorial Island's tightly packed buildings.

Settings > Audio > General. Adjust Master / Sound effect / Music volume sliders to preference (also area music mode, looping, unlock messages, hit sounds). Purely a preference setting -- the wiki lists the sliders but recommends no particular value; no speedrun-specific audio guidance exists.

steps.instruction: Set controls and learn the dialogue-skip techniques
steps.detail: Settings > Controls: toggle Run via the minimap orb, or leave the default Control-key run/walk toggle -- largely moot on the island (short walks, no run-energy pressure) but worth setting once since it carries to the mainland. The two concrete wiki-grounded speedrun techniques for the dialogue-heavy instructors ahead: press Spacebar to auto-select 'Click here to continue', and press a number key to instantly pick the matching numbered dialogue option (mouse-clicking works identically but is slower). Grounding-gap disclosure: no wiki page titled a 'Tutorial Island speedrun guide' exists -- the wiki's only 'Speedrunning' article covers the seasonal Quest Speedrunning minigame, whose own Restrictions section states the tutorial is SKIPPED there entirely (players spawn directly in Lumbridge), so it grounds nothing about in-tutorial pathing/dialogue. These two controls, plus the Skippy NPC full-skip bypass (a lucky, non-reliable trivia -- see the Lumbridge-arrival step), are the only wiki-grounded speedrun-relevant material found for step 0; everything beyond is left '??' rather than invented.

hints.note: Wiki-grounded dialogue-skip techniques (Game_controls.s5) -- the only sourced speedrun material for Tutorial Island besides Skippy.

steps.detail: Exit the starting house through the door and left-click a tile on the ground to walk the path toward the Survival Expert -- click-to-move is the base movement verb from here on; arrow keys pan/rotate the camera (keep the map north-centred so as not to set off in the wrong direction).

Talk to Brynna, the Survival Expert, to receive a small fishing net, then use it on the nearby fishing spot to catch raw shrimps (Fishing xp). Instructions appear top-left; the skills interface shows current level/xp per skill.

steps.instruction: Chop a log and light a fire
steps.detail: Talk to Brynna again to receive a bronze axe and a tinderbox. Use the axe on the nearby tree to collect logs (Woodcutting xp), then use the tinderbox on the logs to light a fire (Firemaking xp). Nearby oak trees are visible but not choppable (need Woodcutting 15, above the level-3 island cap).

Use the raw shrimps on the lit fire to cook them (Cooking xp); eating them afterward restores health but is optional -- health cannot reach 0 on Tutorial Island. Almost every survival skill here caps at level 3 and everything gathered is lost on leaving the island, so a speedrun-efficient line does the single required gather/cook/eat cycle and moves on rather than farming extra shrimps.

steps.instruction: Bake bread with the Master Chef
steps.detail: Enter Building two (the kitchen) and talk to Lev, the Master Chef, to receive a pot of flour and a bucket of water. Use the pot of flour on the bucket of water (or vice versa) to make bread dough, then use the dough on the cooking range to bake bread (Cooking xp). '??' flag: the wiki's own wording on whether Lev replenishes ingredients only after the music tutorial is internally unclear -- not resolved here per own-words/'??' discipline; it doesn't block progression since dough->bread only needs one pass.

steps.instruction: Play a track on the Music Player
steps.detail: Open the Music Player in the kitchen building and select/play a track to complete the prompt. Reads as optional but is a required gate, not a detour -- completing it unlocks emotes and the run-toggle option going forward.

steps.instruction: Talk to the Quest Guide and enter the dungeon
steps.detail: Enter Building three (the quest house) and talk to the Quest Guide, who explains the quest-journal colour code (red = not started, yellow = in progress, green = finished) and the quest-start map icon shown on the minimap/world map. Go down the ladder into the Tutorial Island Dungeon -- shared location for both the Mining Instructor and Combat Instructor ahead.

steps.instruction: Mine, smelt and smith a bronze dagger
steps.detail: Talk to Dezzick, the Mining Instructor, to receive a bronze pickaxe. Mine the nearby copper rock and tin rock (Mining xp each), then use the furnace to smelt the copper + tin ore into a bronze bar (Smithing xp). Receive a hammer from Dezzick and use it on the bronze bar at an anvil to smith a bronze dagger (Smithing xp). The wiki calls out pushing both Mining and Smithing to level 3 here as worthwhile -- almost nowhere else has ore, a furnace and an anvil this close together -- but extra reps cost time a speedrun-efficient line doesn't need.

steps.instruction: Kill a giant rat with melee
steps.detail: Talk to Vannaka, the Combat Instructor. Equip the bronze dagger (opens the worn-equipment interface), receive a bronze sword and wooden shield, then open the combat-options interface and pick an attack style (accurate=Attack, aggressive=Strength, defensive=Defence xp; or unarmed punch/kick/block for the same three). Attack and kill one giant rat in the rat pit. Health cannot reach 0 here (floors at 1) so eating is not required; the wiki also documents a 2-damage-then-retreat loop to farm extra melee xp by re-entering combat repeatedly, but that is a deliberate detour, not part of the fastest line. Hitpoints cannot be trained on Tutorial Island.

steps.instruction: Kill a giant rat with Ranged
steps.detail: Talk to Vannaka again to receive a shortbow and 50 bronze arrows. Equip the shortbow and kill one giant rat with Ranged (melee re-entry to the pit is blocked at this point). Talk to Vannaka a third time to close out the combat lesson (combat stats, melee/ranged recap, Wilderness overview).

Enter Building four (the Tutorial Island bank) and use a bank booth to open the bank interface and note the deposit/withdraw mechanics -- no items actually move here. The poll booth is on view but cannot actually be used to vote (it needs a higher total level than obtainable on the island, max 57); Tutorial Island's bank tellers themselves cannot be talked to or traded with, unlike normal bank tellers.

The Account Guide (Financial Advisor) in the bank building gives an Account Management menu overview: membership, worlds/world-switcher, store, bonds, inbox, name changer. Also mentions account safety (bank PIN, authenticator) -- no in-game action, redirects to Customer Support.

Enter Building five (the chapel) and talk to Brother Brace, the Prayer instructor, for the Prayer list, prayer points and recharge explanation. Bury any bones on hand for Prayer xp -- only possible if bones were picked up earlier in the rat pen, otherwise Prayer training is unavailable again until the mainland. The organ upstairs is playable but non-essential -- skip it on a speedrun-efficient line.

Talk to Paul, the Ironman tutor, on the road to the final building, and choose an Ironman mode (Standard / Hardcore / Ultimate / Group) or remain a normal account. Mode is freely switchable while still on the island; after leaving, Adam in Lumbridge only allows downward switches (Ultimate/Hardcore -> Standard -> non-Ironman), and the choice locks permanently after one week. '??': the wiki does not detail the Group Ironman branch's internal steps beyond noting Group Ironman players continue to The Node south of the final building instead of the Magic building.

Enter Building six (the Magic building) and talk to Terrova, the Magic Instructor, to receive 5 air runes and 5 mind runes. Open the spellbook interface, select Wind Strike, and cast it on a nearby chicken (Magic xp). Only one guaranteed cast is allowed here before being told to move on; the wiki notes players can return to the rat pit to push Magic to level 3 (up to 32 of each rune, since xp is granted even on a miss) -- an optional detour, not part of the fastest line.

steps.instruction: Cast Lumbridge Home Teleport to leave the island
steps.detail: Talk to Terrova again. If planning Ironman Mode and it wasn't chosen yet, he asks before you leave -- backtrack to the Ironman tutor first if needed. Cast Lumbridge Home Teleport from the spellbook (auto-taught, no Magic level or rune cost, ~30 min real-time cooldown) to leave Tutorial Island.

hints.note: Only teleport a fresh account has; ~30 min cooldown, free of Magic level/rune cost.

steps.detail: Land in Lumbridge, north-east of Tutorial Island, with standard starting provisions. Spawn point branches on the Gielinor Guide choice (see the character-creation-adjacent dialogue step): outside The Sheared Ram pub near Adventurer Jon -- who immediately introduces Adventure Paths -- if Adventure Paths was unlocked and Ironman Mode was not chosen; inside/near the walled Lumbridge Castle courtyard otherwise. This closes step 0 (Tutorial Island) -- mainland progression is a separate domain. Closing aside, not part of the baseline route: an NPC named Skippy occasionally spawns in the starting house near the Gielinor Guide and can skip the entire tutorial outright, but he isn't permanently present (an anti-bot measure), so this is a lucky bypass rather than a reliable line. Trivia: island-wide caps are max total level 57, max total xp 3334.

steps.instruction: Land in Lumbridge and unlock world chat
steps.detail: Casting Lumbridge Home Teleport off the island permanently unlocks world chat and further skill training. Ironman-mode players are diverted to a separate Ironman tutor before the teleport.

steps.instruction: Buy a spade at the Lumbridge General Store
steps.detail:
First gear stop: Lumbridge General Store in the castle courtyard -- a non-GE named vendor that restocks on a per-item timer (sellmultiplier=1300/buymultiplier=400/delta=30). Buy a Spade (stock 5) here first -- it's mandatory for X Marks the Spot and not obtainable free anywhere else nearby. Shears (stock 2), Pot (stock 5), Bucket (stock 3) and Tinderbox (stock 2) are also sold here as backups, but Sheep Shearer/Cook's Assistant each hand out or spawn their own copies free during the quest, so only the Spade is a hard buy.

Travel budget for the first hour is walking only: Lumbridge Home Teleport (already used to arrive) resets you back to the castle courtyard, not useful for skipping travel elsewhere since it's the hub you start in. Every other Lumbridge transport option (amulet of glory, gnome glider, fairy ring, ring of returning, canoe) needs an item/quest/level not yet available. The Al Kharid gate costs 10 coins each way until Prince Ali Rescue is done (avoidable via a long detour) -- worth knowing as a coin sink to route around, though none of the four openers ahead require crossing it.

Opener order ahead (own-synthesis from each quest's own Details/startmap + Lumbridge's Quests list -- not from a dedicated speedrun source): Cook's Assistant and Sheep Shearer first, since both start/finish inside or immediately around Lumbridge Castle (kitchen, cellar, cow field, mill, sheep pen, 1st-floor spinning wheel all within ~10-30 tiles) with zero long-distance travel and a shared turn-in visit; The Restless Ghost third, since its only detour is the one-way Wizards' Tower basement trip and the rest (chapel/graveyard/swamp hut) stays Lumbridge-local; X Marks the Spot last, since its dig route is the only opener that pushes travel out to Draynor Village and Falador before finishing at Port Sarim, doubling as the first venture into the wider road network later quests need, with its 200gp + free-skill-lamp reward as a fitting capstone once the cheaper three are banked. Gap flag: no Xerxes/DunkingOreos speedrunner notes exist in this project's wiki-kb study set (those are YouTube creators, not wiki pages, so wikicli-only discipline can't source them) -- this ordering is grounded directly in each quest's own wiki page, not cross-checked against a dedicated speedrun source; flagging so a future agent with those notes can override it.
One-time low-tier gather before the prayer-pot-supply supply loop is established. Use drops (Hill Giants, slayer tasks) or NPC spawns. No rates known — "??" until measured.

A 30-pineapple ultracompost batch (per setup-ultracompost's recipe) needs either two visits ~50min apart or a world-hop to refill a second Trader Crewmember's independent stock (16 dock locations total, per Trader_Crewmember.s1).

steps.detail: Fill compost bins with 30 pineapples then add 15 volcanic ash per bin to produce ultracompost. Ahead-of-time stockpile before herb runs.

mapMarkers.label: Ectofuntus (Port Phasmatys) -- for the step's own location.zone label
Nearest real compost bin, west of Port Phasmatys (Compost_bin.s1 map pin)

hints.note: Direct method (no bin needed): use 2 volcanic ash on 1 bucket of already-made supercompost -> 1 bucket ultracompost, 2 ticks, 1.1 farming xp. Fastest per-bucket path if supercompost buckets are already on hand.

Compost-bin method: bin already containing supercompost + 25 volcanic ash (consumed regardless of bin fill level) -> collect 15 buckets of ultracompost via 15 empty buckets, 10 xp/bucket. Big Compost Bin (Farming Guild, 45 Farming) doubles every number: 30 buckets, 50 ash, 30 buckets to collect.

steps.detail: Withdraw: coins (charter fare + 30 pineapples, total ??), Digsite pendant or Fossil Island transport (??). No other inventory needed — pineapples and ash fill en route.

mapMarkers.label: Fossil Island Volcano (ash-pile mining site, per gather-volcanic-ash)

hints.note: Keep a charged pendant banked separately for repeat runs.

Fossil_Island s1: digsite pendant is the fastest (teleports near the Digsite barge, speak to the barge guard to fast-travel to Museum Camp; can also be charged on the House on the Hill strange machine for a repeatable house-teleport option). Without a pendant: Digsite teleport scroll (lands SW of the barge) or a gnome glider to Lemanto Andra (SW of the barge, reachable via ring of dueling->Emir's Arena glider, Al Kharid gem-trader glider, or a royal seed pod to the Grand Tree glider after Monkey Madness II).

hints.note: Jatix's Herblore Shop Stock table (StoreTableHead sellmultiplier=1000 buymultiplier=700 delta=30): Vial of water stock=750/restock=100s, or buy the Water-filled vial pack (stock=750/restock=50s) for a batched purchase. A single 14-vial brewing run (per pps-05-withdraw-brew's loadout) is far under base stock -- no world-hop needed.

steps.detail: Withdraw: 14 grimy ranarr + 14 vials of water (clean-then-unf in one sitting; two half-inventories per load). Snape grass stays banked until the unf potions are made.

Herb cleaning and unf-mixing are click-paced; pair with bank-adjacent idle.

steps.detail: No requirements. Fred wants 20 balls of wool. Fastest route is simply buying 20 balls of wool (tradeable) and handing them in; the 'intended' route -- shear sheep in the pen with shears, spin the wool at a spinning wheel -- is optional and grants +50 bonus Crafting xp over just buying. Same opener tier as Cook's Assistant, designed to walk new players through Lumbridge.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Novice quest; only real gate is being able to kill or flee a level 13 skeleton. Talk to Father Aereck about the haunted Lumbridge graveyard, head to Father Urhney's hut west of Lumbridge Swamp and talk to him, then go to the small room in the graveyard, equip the Ghostspeak amulet and talk to the coffin-ghost. Go to the Wizards' Tower basement and search the altar for a ghost's skull (a level 13 skeleton spawns but does not need to be killed, just leave), then use the skull on the open coffin to finish. Required for: Animal Magnetism, Curse of the Empty Lord, Ghosts Ahoy, Making History, Nature Spirit, Creature of Fenkenstrain.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No requirements, tutorial-tier treasure hunt intro quest. Talk to Veos in the Sheared Ram pub, Lumbridge, to start. Dig at 4 marked spots in sequence: by Bob's Brilliant Axes (west of the plant against the wall), behind Lumbridge Castle near the kitchen door, north-west of the Draynor Village jail by the wheat farm, and in the pig pen next to Martin the Master Gardener. Use the resulting ancient casket on Veos at the Port Sarim docks to finish.

Track down a crop-raiding beast for farmer Sarah at the Falador farm: follow a trail of stolen vegetables to a hole near Draynor Manor, investigate a skeleton underground, get reinforced goggles from Spria, then kill the level-37 Sourhog and bring its foot back as proof.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Requires the ability to defeat level 45+ enemies (no formal skill/quest gate). Instanced combat gauntlet down a rift near the Digsite: fill a rage bar killing angry unicorns/bears/rats/goblins with themed weapons in room 1, hunt 5-6 fear reapers hiding in holes in room 2, tell 6 confusion beasts apart from their fakes (fakes vanish after 8 hits, give free combat xp) in room 3, kill hopeless creatures 3 times each in room 4, then defeat all 3 heads of Tolna to finish. Progress resets on logout mid-room; re-entering the rift post-quest also completes a Varrock Medium diary task.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Novice quest, opens the troll questline. Talk to Denulth about the troll raids on Burthorpe; ask Eohric on the 1st floor of the castle then Harold at the Burthorpe bar about the missing guard. Give Harold an Asgarnian ale (a Blurberry special auto-wins the dice bet, otherwise gamble over 60gp repeatedly until he goes bankrupt) for an IOU note with a combination; use the combination on the 5 coloured balls at the castle to unlock a door. Talk to Saba in the cave north-west of Burthorpe about another route up the plateau; talk to Tenzing, then Dunstan for a certificate, and exchange the certificate + climbing boots + iron bar for spiked boots. Give Tenzing 10 unnoted bread + 10 unnoted trout + the spiked boots for a map, walk the back route around the mountain (ignore the Death Plateau warning message on the main path), and talk to Denulth with the combination to finish. Required for: Troll Stronghold.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No requirements. Doric wants an errand run for anvil access: buy or mine (with the bronze pickaxe he gives you) 2 iron ore + 4 copper ore + 6 clay, all un-noted, from the Dwarven Mine south of Ice Mountain just east of the start point, then hand them to Doric. One of the short prerequisite quests feeding into Devious Minds and the Falador diary.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Classic quest, gates the Rune platebody and a huge amount of mid-game content. Get Melzar's, Thalzar's, and Lozar's map pieces to Crandor in any order: Thalzar's requires using silk+lobster pot+unfired bowl+wizard's mind bomb on a magic door in the Dwarven Mine; Melzar's requires clearing his eponymous maze in Rimmington, killing a sequence of monsters (zombie rat, ghost, skeleton, zombies, Melzar the Mad, then a level-82 lesser demon) to collect keys; Lozar's is bought from or telegrabbed off Wormbrain in Port Sarim's jail. Combine the three pieces, buy/repair a boat with 3 planks+90 steel nails+2,000 coins from Klarense, sail to Crandor via Ned in Draynor Village, and kill the level-83 dragon Elvarg with an anti-dragon shield equipped (antifire potion recommended). Report back to Oziach in Edgeville to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Short novice quest that unlocks the dwarf multicannon, one of the most impactful early combat-XP tools in the game. Repair 6 bent railings on the west/south fence for Lawgof with a hammer, retrieve dwarf remains from a tower, then find the goblin Lollk hiding in a crate deep in a cave south-east of the Fishing Guild. Reassemble a broken multicannon in the correct tool order (hook on spring, pliers on safety switch, tooth tool on gear), then travel to the Dwarven Black Guard camp to get Nulodion's notes and a cannonball mould before returning to Lawgof to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Third quest in the Mahjarrat series. Lady Servil's family has been kidnapped by General Khazard to fight in his arena. Get Khazard armour from a chest in a house south of Ardougne Monastery and wear it to be let into the prison. Free Sammy Servil after buying Khali brew (5 coins) from the Khazard barman to get the Head Guard drunk and steal his cell keys. Fight through a scripted sequence in the arena: level 63 Khazard ogre, level 44 Khazard scorpion, level 137 Bouncer (hellhound) -- all safespottable behind a skeleton, or use Protect from Melee. The optional level 142 General Khazard fight can be skipped via the door's quick-escape. Return to Lady Servil to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No skill/quest requirements, no combat. Goblins are debating a new armour colour. Collect 3 sets of goblin mail from crates around Goblin Village (or off goblin drops), make blue dye (woad leaves from Wyson the gardener + Aggie in Draynor) and orange dye (combine red+yellow dye from Aggie), dye one mail orange and one blue, leave the third plain. Show the generals orange (rejected), then blue (rejected) -- they settle on the original plain brown. This is the real gate on the RFD 'Freeing the Goblin generals' subquest.

steps.detail: Second Mahjarrat-series quest, novice, with a good/evil branch (same structure as Temple of Ikov). Ceril Carnillean says cultists stole his family armour; head to the cave south-east of the Ardougne clock tower and talk to Clivet to learn about the Mahjarrat Hazeel. Choose a side: siding with Ceril means refusing Clivet, turning 5 sewer valves in specific directions to reach the hideout raft, killing Alomone (level 13) for Carnillean armour, then reporting to Ceril for a fake ending -- search the upstairs cupboard for Hazeel's mark and poison and show Ceril to fire the butler for the real reward. Siding with Hazeel means accepting Clivet's offer, poisoning Ceril's cooking range (accidentally kills the dog Scruffy instead), getting Hazeel's mark from Clivet, turning the same 5 valves, then helping Butler Jones steal a restoration scroll from a locked chest in Ceril's house and giving it to Alomone to begin the resurrection. Choice also has a minor effect on Secrets of the North.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Short experienced-tier miniquest. The Strange Old Man asks you to retrieve a strange icon guarded by the six Barrows Brothers — defeat all six, then loot the icon from the Barrows reward chest (keep at least 6 free inventory slots or you must redo all six brothers to get it), and return it to him to finish. Wiki lists 'Required for completing: Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire' (Twisted Tale/lore cross-reference, not a normal prerequisite chain). Promoted into this batch from a stalled wave3:morytania-prayer-pvm queue entry per DEPTH_BACKLOG Band C guidance.

steps.detail: Must own a cat and beat a level-91 Possessed Priest plus one randomly-assigned level 75-81 elemental guardian (Apmeken/Crondis/Scabaras magic, or Het melee - determined by which canopic jar you're given). Give the Wanderer a tinderbox+waterskin(4), enter the pyramid (no teleporting out once inside - aggressive lvl-84 mummies + poison scarabs, use minimap-click nav to dodge traps, jump the pit at 20%+ run energy). Solve a golden-bird door puzzle to trigger flashback 1. Drop your cat to answer the Sphinx's riddle (answer: 9) or lose the cat, then see the High Priest. Flashback 2: re-enter pyramid, take the correct canopic jar from the west room, kill the guardian that spawns, take the jar out (crossing the pit twice), then return and drop the jar back before leaving. Prep for the ceremony: give the Embalmer linen+salt+sap, give the Carpenter willow logs for a holy symbol. Flashback 3: use the holy symbol on a sarcophagus in the east room, kill the level-91 Possessed Priest, talk High Priest. Flashback 4: talk High Priest again in the temple to finish. Post-quest: equipping the catspeak amulet and asking your cat about "being confused" unlocks bonus lore cutscenes. High spine value: opens Sophanem/Menaphos region access.

Report all 3 findings back to Jorral, then relay them to King Lathas.

Use all three keys on the final blue door, dodge the hidden killer's knife throws by aiming a mirror at their wardrobe, then fight and unmask Abigale as the killer.

Once all 3 for a display are answered correctly its questions are replaced by the creature's common+scientific name. Return to Orlando after all 14 displays are done for the xp reward.

steps.detail: Edmond's daughter Elena vanished into plague-locked West Ardougne. His wife Alrena makes a gas mask from dwellberries; soften the mud behind the house with 4 buckets of water then dig under the wall with the spade, use rope on the blocked pipe grill to get through. Inside, show Jethick Elena's picture, learn from the Rehnison family she was taken to the barred plague house. Cure Bravek's hangover (mix chocolate dust + bucket of milk + snape grass) to get a warrant past the Mourners guarding that house, find and free Elena, then return to Edmond via the manhole. One of the Ardougne-diary prereq quests alongside Sheep Shearer and Clock Tower.

steps.detail: Requires beating level-69 Draugen and an unarmed/unarmoured fight vs Koschei the deathless (first 3 forms; 4th form is safe to "die" to, even on HCIM). Order: lose a drinking contest to Manni, get Hunters' Talisman from Sigli, talk Olaf, obtain a lyre (craft from swaying-tree branch via Lalli's stew quest-within-quest, or farm Lanzig's 1/17 drop). Spin golden wool in Seers' Village if crafting the lyre, buy low-alcohol keg from Poison Salesman. Back in Rellekka: use beer on council workman for strange object, enchant lyre with raw fish at a strange altar near the bridge, hunt the Draugen via talisman, light strange object and use on longhall pipe, play enchanted lyre on stage, then win the rematch drinking contest vs Manni with the low-alcohol keg swap. Sigmund/Swensen trial is a long NPC-chase-and-recall-order chain culminating in paying Askeladden 5,000 coins then repeating the NPC list in reverse order. Thorvald trial: bank everything (no weapons/armour/runes allowed), craft a dramen/celastrus staff underground, fight Koschei's first 3 forms unarmed. Peer the Seer trial: bank all bankable items, solve a riddle (each line gives one letter of a 4-letter word) then a multi-step item-transformation puzzle (bucket/jug/vase/disks/red herring) to unlock a trapdoor exit. Finish by reporting to Brundt. High spine value: gates Fremennik Isles (Neitiznot/Jatizso/Miscellania/Etceteria) sea access.

Climb into the stronghold over the crumbled wall, ignore or kill the level 48 Khazard commanders, take an orb of protection from the upstairs chest, exit, and report to Elkoy to be sent to the village centre. Track down and kill the level 112 Khazard warlord north-west of the battlefield (safespottable near the West Ardougne wall) for the other two orbs, then return all 3 orbs to King Bolren to finish. Required for: Monkey Madness I, The Path of Glouphrie, Medium Western Provinces Diary, Medium Varrock Diary.

Enter the Tree Gnome Village maze/dungeon (worth starting Tree Gnome Village here too), find a key in a crate, unlock the west gate, and get Glarial's pebble from Golrie (keep it banked for Roving Elves later). Bank everything except the pebble, food, and a teleport method, use the pebble on Glarial's Tombstone east of the Waterfall to enter, and retrieve Glarial's amulet and urn from the tomb. Return with rope + 6 air/water/earth runes + amulet + urn: cross the raft/rope route into the waterfall dungeon, unlock the door past the Fire Giants, place one of each rune on 3 pillars, wear the amulet at Glarial's statue to raise the ground, then pour the urn into the chalice to finish. Talk to Elissa at the Digsite, read Varmen's notes in the Exam Centre bookcase, then talk to Curator Haig Halen at Varrock Museum about the Uzer statuette, pickpocket him for the display cabinet key, and take the statuette from an upstairs case. Return to Uzer, place the statuette in an empty alcove, rotate 4 statues to all face north to open the portal north; optionally chisel the throne for 2 each of sapphire/emerald/ruby. Make a phoenix quill pen (feather + black dye) and write the golem program on papyrus, then use the strange implement on the golem followed immediately by the program to finish. Region-opener with no known deeper dependency; sets up the desert quest line (Uzer).

steps.detail: First Elemental Workshop-series quest. Search a bookcase south-west of the Seers' Village bank for a battered book and read it to start. Slash it with a knife to get a battered key, then use the key on an odd-looking wall in the northern anvil-icon building to enter the underground Elemental Workshop. Turn the east then west water controls green and pull the lever to start the water wheel; fix the bellows with leather+needle (Crafting 20) and pull its lever. Mine an elemental rock (Mining 20) to spawn and kill an earth elemental (level 35) for elemental ore (kill extras now to save time for Elemental Workshop II / a Mind shield later). Fill a stone bowl with lava and use it on the furnace, then smelt the ore with 4 coal into elemental metal (Smithing 20) and smith an elemental shield at the workbench with the slashed book in inventory.

steps.detail: Second Elemental Workshop-series quest. Find a beaten book with a scroll in the Exam Centre library south of the Digsite, read both, then head to the Elemental Workshop (same entry as EW1). Search machinery in the water elemental room for a hatch key (must have read the scroll first), and smelt 2-3 elemental ore into elemental metal bars beforehand. Downstairs, collect crane and lever schematics, craft a crane claw from one bar, connect a junction box's pipes per the lever schematic's clue, and find 3 randomly-placed cogs + a pipe piece in crates to fix a broken pipe and set cog sizes on the wind tunnel pins (small upper-left, medium lower-left, large right). Run each elemental metal bar through the crane-lava-press-water tank-fan sequence via a cart-advancing lever to produce a primed bar (do this twice for a Mind shield). One level further down, drain a bar into a primed mind bar at the extractor gun/hat (temporarily lowers Magic by 20 per bar powered). With the beaten book in inventory, smith the primed bar into a mind helmet to finish; a second powered bar plus the slashed book from Elemental Workshop I in inventory instead makes a mind shield.

steps.detail: One-way point of no return dungeon crawl west of Ardougne toward Tirannwn. Talk King Lathas then Koftik at West Ardougne cave entrance, enter dungeon (no going back except via lever/rope pull). Light an arrow via Koftik's cloth+tinderbox, shoot the bridge guide-rope, cross grid maze carefully (keep HP>15), pull lever. Collect 4 light orbs (avoid wall traps, use plank on flat rocks, disarm trap on 4th), fuel furnace, descend well. Navigate slave-cage/pipe area (50 Thieving shortcut optional). Get unicorn horn via railing-on-boulder trick, kill 3 paladins (safespottable) for badges, use badges+horn on well to open door. Meet dwarves Niloof and Klank (get gauntlets - keep inv slot free), steal Witch's cat to enter her house for loot, kill 3 demons for amulets, brew dwarf beer + tinderbox on tomb. Kill Kalrag the spider (safespottable, ignores Protect Melee), get Iban's dove wearing Klank's gauntlets. Kill a Disciple of Iban for ceremonial robes (required to enter temple), use Doll of Iban on the well in Iban's temple (repeat through his attacks, ~8dmg unblockable spikes) to destroy him. Return to King Lathas via Koftik. Gates access to Tirannwn/Elf lands questline infrastructure.

steps.detail: Novice Hunter-skill quest, no series. Charlie the zookeeper needs help finding huntsman Nickolaus, missing near Eagles' Peak west of the Tree Gnome Stronghold. Find his tent, pick up a bird book that drops a metal feather, and use it to open the mountain-top door into Eagles' Peak Dungeon. Shout to Nickolaus, trapped by a giant eagle, then craft two eagle costumes (10 eagle feathers + swamp tar + yellow dye + 50 coins at Varrock's Fancy Dress Shop) so you can both disguise as eagles. To open the eagle door to Nickolaus you first need three feathers: bronze (defeat a net trap via four corner winches), silver (Hunter 27 to read a pedestal trail leading to a hidden tunnel, then kill or threaten a level 13 kebbit), and gold (solve a bird-seed/lever puzzle moving eagle statues and wing-gates through a specific lever/feeder sequence, reset lever available if you mess it up). Use all three feathers on the eagle door, wear the disguise to pass the guard eagle, then learn to catch ferrets from Nickolaus (no box trap needed for this part) and deliver a ferret to Charlie at Ardougne Zoo to finish.

steps.detail: No formal quest prerequisites, but recreating King Alvis's battleaxe needs Thurgo (Mudskipper Point smith) which can be found either via The Knight's Sword or by asking the Keldagrim librarian then Reldo in Varrock library (no quest completion actually required). Introduces Keldagrim city. Flow: talk Dwarven Boatman, then Commander Veldaban, then Blasidar the sculptor (east Keldagrim) who needs exquisite clothes+boots+a repaired dwarven battleaxe for a statue. Clothes: get a costume book from a bookcase in the library (need <30kg weight to climb), buy coal/logs-spun cloth from Vermundi for 200gp. Boots: steal the left boot from Dromund when he's not looking, telegrab the right boot through his window. Axe: get it from Santiri's weapon shop, socket 3 sapphires, take to Thurgo at Mudskipper Point with an iron bar to repair (may need a redberry pie bribe). Deliver all 3 items to Riki (Blasidar's model) then talk Blasidar for a cutscene. Join the Consortium: pick one company (not Red Axe) upstairs in the market, run ore-delivery fetch-tasks for their secretary (hidden score, 20 points per success, need 100 to progress, wearing full H.A.M. robes gives Purple Pewter a +20 head start), then bar-delivery tasks for the director, then formally join the company and report back to Commander Veldaban to finish. Do not world-hop mid-consortium-grind or progress is lost.

steps.detail: Talk to Bert in Yanille (house next to the POH portal) to get a sandy hand. Buy/bring a beer and give it to the Guard Captain at the Dragon Inn pub south of Bert's house. Ring the bell outside the Wizards' Guild (or enter with 66 Magic) and give the hand to Zavistic Rarve. Get Bert's rota from Bert, travel to Brimhaven, find Sandy south of the Shrimp and Parrot restaurant, search his desk for Sandy's rota, and pickpocket him for sand. Back in Yanille, get a magic scroll from Bert, ring the bell for a magical orb from Zavistic Rarve, who then offers to teleport you to Port Sarim. At Betty's magic shop get bottled water, mix in redberries then white berries (order matters) for pink dye, dye the lantern lens for a rose-tinted lens, then use the lens on the counter from the doorway for a truth serum, and hand over sand. In Brimhaven, distract Sandy with dialogue and use the truth serum on his coffee mug, then activate the magical orb and click through every dialogue option with him. Back in Yanille, ring the bell twice more at the Wizards' Guild (getting a cutscene on the second), sail to Entrana and talk to Mazion at the sandpit for a wizard's head, then return to Yanille and ring the bell/talk to Zavistic Rarve one last time to finish.

steps.detail: Two-trip Entrana quest that unlocks the balloon transport network -- explicitly a hard prerequisite for Monkey Madness II's Gnome Stronghold route. Trip 1: gather papyrus/candle/dye/silk/sand-filled sacks around Entrana to help Auguste build a balloon frame. Trip 2 (after leaving to gather logs/willow branches, since axes can't be brought to Entrana): attach 12 willow branches and 10 regular logs to the balloon. Piloting is a 3-screen minigame of dropping sandbags, burning logs, pulling coloured ropes, and "relaxing" a set number of times per prompt to control altitude/direction -- crashing just costs 10 more logs and a restart from Auguste. Optionally stockpile extra noted logs (oak/willow/yew/magic) in log storage to unlock further balloon routes post-quest.

steps.detail: Long quest, mostly farming-timer-gated. Talk to Ellamaria then the Wise Old Man in Draynor (wearing ring of charos) to pass a quiz. Grow seeds for 5 NPCs to trade up to rarer seeds: Lyra (west of Port Phasmatys, fairy ring AJQ) grows onions for orchid seeds; Kragen (north Ardougne, fairy ring BJR) grows cabbages for snowdrop seeds; Elstan (south Falador) grows marigolds for delphinium seeds; Dantaera (Catherby) needs a cutting from the White Tree on Ice Mountain grown in a filled plant pot; Brother Althric (Edgeville monastery) needs the ring of charos(a) thrown down the well then retrieved with a fishing rod, plus rune dust used on a plant cure for diseased vines, for rose seeds; Bernald (behind Burthorpe pub) similarly needs a plant-cure/rune-dust vine cure via Alain at the Taverley tree patch, for vine seeds. Then move two statues via trolley (Lumbridge king statue east across the bridge to the garden's east plinth; Falador Saradomin statue north through the gate, cutscene, to the south plinth). Finally plant everything with compost/supercompost in the garden (10-15 min grow, cannot die), talk to Ellamaria, then King Roald (ring of charos(a) must be equipped or he won't follow) for the finishing cutscene.

steps.detail: Firemaking 45 is listed as a requirement but is not actually needed in practice (only used to identify brazier fuel by inspection; trial-and-error works without it). Build a statue for Lazim from 32kg sandstone base + 20kg sandstone body (chiseled down from bigger pieces), pick a head design, carve it from a 5kg granite piece, hand it over and fall into a buried temple. Bottom floor: chisel a fallen statue for 4 limbs, collect M/R/K/Z sigils by walking the outer hallway rooms (limbs auto-consumed to open each door), place all 4 sigils in the center room. Middle floor: mold soft clay + carved 5kg granite into a new stone head for the pedestal (triggers cutscene), then solve 4 corner puzzles - feed food to Pentyn (NW), cast a wind spell on the furnace (NE), light 6 braziers with correct fuel per item (SE), cast a fire spell on the fountain (SW) - to pass the magic barrier. Top floor: cast Crumble Undead on a boneguard, then Protect-from-Melee-tank while talking to a second boneguard, patch a wall with 3 sandstone pieces (chisel each into place) to finish. Region-opener quest, no known deeper dependency; unlocks quick desert-temple teleport infrastructure via Camulet.

steps.detail: Direct sequel to Dwarf Cannon. Cross the river to Dondakan via the Dwarven Ferryman, then collect 3 Dwarven lore book pages in the Dwarven Mine (kill scorpions, search mine carts, mine any non-Adamantite/Coal rock). Make a golden cannonball (gold bar smelted at a furnace) and fire it at Dondakan's rock to get a Schematic; combine it with base schematics from the lore book and further schematics from the Dwarf Engineer, then smith a gold helmet. Final battle: equip the gold helmet + best pickaxe, mine 6 or 15 gold ore inside the arena (15 lowers the boss from level 125 to 75), then fight one of three Arzinian Avatar variants (chosen by your highest combat stat) within an 8-minute time limit -- removing the helmet mid-fight aborts the attempt. Talk to Dondakan again to finish.

steps.detail: Must beat 3 level-75 golems (white/grey/black), each weak to a different melee style (stab/slash/crush respectively) or a ranged/magic water-channel puzzle. Talk Awusah then Ghaslor the Elder for the ballad, find torn robe top+bottom in a cupboard, repair with needle+thread and equip (Robes of Elidinis), telegrab an ancestral key. Follow the river north to a waterfall cave, rope the overhanging root, use the ancestral key wearing the robes to open the door. Kill white golem (stab) then clear its water channel via Thieving; kill grey golem (slash) then clear via pickaxe; kill black golem (crush) then clear via Ranged/Magic on a target. Talk to a spirit through the north door, return to Nardah. Separately: take a genie's shoes by Awusah's door, knife them for a sole, find a crevice west of the general store, rope it, talk to and strike a deal with the Genie for a statuette, then place the statuette on the shrine plinth north of the fountain to finish. Region-opener (Nardah/desert), no known deeper dependency; useful all-purpose restoration shrine unlock.

Go back through the north gate, search-and-pull another lever (needs 42 Thieving), fight through to the Fire Warrior of Lesarkus (level 84, kill with ice arrows, Protect from Magic negates damage), then hand Winelda 20 unnoted limpwurt roots at once to be teleported across the lava (one-way, no going back - caution for ironmen). Optionally grab a shiny key for a dungeon shortcut. Push through to the Guardians of Armadyl room and pick a side to finish.

steps.detail: Grish asks you to investigate zombified ogres (zogres) infesting the ogre ceremonial grounds. Head into the cave below Jiggig, being careful of disease from zogre/skogre attacks if under 89 combat (carry super restores). Loot a ruined backpack and a torn page from a skeleton/lectern, then pry open a coffin with a knife for a black prism. Head to Yanille, use the recovered Dragon Inn tankard on the bartender there, then visit Zavistic Rarve at the Wizards' Guild. Investigate the ogres' bedridden neighbor Sithik Ints: search his room for a portraiture book, necromancy book, papyrus/charcoal and a Book of 'H.A.M.', read and use all three on him, then sketch a portrait that reads as an honest "classic realist charcoal portrait" (higher Crafting speeds this up) and get it signed by the Dragon Inn bartender. Bring the necromancy book, H.A.M. book, tankard and signed portrait to Zavistic Rarve for a strange potion; spike Sithik's tea with it, then question him for the secrets to Relicym's balm, curing the disease, and removing the undead ogres. Return to Grish for an ogre gate key and to learn comp ogre bow use, then fight the level-111 Slash Bash (uses Ranged+Melee — safespot with Protect from Missiles, or use Crumble Undead) in the ceremonial chamber to finish.

steps.detail: Search the shelf in the ruined SW house of Mort'ton and read the diary found there to start (this also unlocks making Serum 207). Make 2 doses of Serum 207 (clean tarromin + vial of water, then add ashes), use one on Razmire (afflicted) in the general store, kill 5 loar shades for their remains, return the remains to Razmire. Buy an olive oil from his general store and 5 timber beams/5 limestone bricks/25 swamp paste (+ optional Flamtaer hammer) from his builders' store. Serum the sick Ulsquire Shauncy east of the store and talk to him twice about the remains/temple. Head to the temple NE of Mort'ton and repair it to 100% with at least 20 sanctity (minigame, safer with other players present), light the sacred altar (or wait for someone with 5+ Firemaking), pour olive oil on the flame for sacred oil, and dip Serum 207 in the flame for permanent-cure Serum 208. Make a pyre log with the sacred oil, take it plus loar remains to one of the stone funeral pyre groups west of the general store, light the pyre (25 Prayer xp), collect the reward, then cure both Razmire and Ulsquire with Serum 208 to finish.

steps.detail: Talk to an examiner to attempt (and auto-fail) a first exam, then get a sealed letter from Curator Haig Halen at Varrock Museum. North of the Exam Centre near the blue urn hilltop, search bushes for a teddy bear, take a panning tray, use a cup of tea on the panning guide, pan for a Special cup (and an opal if needed), and pickpocket the nearby workman for an animal skull and specimen brush. Deliver the teddy/cup/skull to the three named students around the dig (NW, E, S respectively, talk twice each). Retake all 3 exams at the Exam Centre picking the correct dialogue options (fail is not possible once you know the answers) to get Level 1/2/3 certificates, re-visiting the students between exams (the female student demands an opal for the 2nd). Save the certificates for the museum curator. Back at the Exam Centre's western room, get a rock pick and specimen jar from cupboards, use the trowel on soil in a level-3 dig site until you find an ancient talisman (can take a while), then talk to Terry Balando and use his invitation on any digsite workman except the one by the western winch. Rope down the western winch to grab arcenia root and search bricks, then rope down the northern winch to talk to Doug Deeping about the rocks. South of the site, unlock a chest for chemical powder, get an unidentified liquid from a barrel (careful — it deals 25 damage and breaks the vial if dropped), grind charcoal, then give the powder/liquid to Terry Balando and mix nitroglycerin+ammonium nitrate, then charcoal, then arcenia root into a chemical compound. Use the compound then a tinderbox on the bricks down the western winch, enter the chamber (watch for level-22 skeletons), grab the stone tablet, and hand it to Terry Balando to finish.
